claim_boundary: This began as a timestamped risk watch for wildfire spread potential. It is now verified only as an incident-report candidate; satellite burn-scar confirmation is still a separate evidence step.
risk_area_label: Southern High Plains: eastern New Mexico into western Texas
task_text: Watch the SPC Day 2 critical fire-weather corridor across eastern New Mexico and western Texas for new smoke plume, active-fire, or burn-scar evidence after the valid window.
deduction: Official SPC Day 2 Fire Weather Outlook marks a critical area for eastern New Mexico and western Texas.
Forecast ingredients include dry fuels, relative humidity near 10-20%, and wind corridors capable of supporting fire spread.
The watch becomes a future catch only if independent post-window evidence confirms a new fire, smoke plume, thermal anomaly, or burn scar inside the bbox.

steps: Query NASA FIRMS or NIFC incident feeds for new detections/incidents in the bbox after the valid window.
If a candidate exists, seed Sentinel-2 imagery using explicit pre-event and post-event windows.
Retag candidate frames with the local Ollama Qwen vision model and mark the row verified only if visual evidence supports it.

verification_result.summary: NM Fire Info reported the Sparks Fire in Quay County, New Mexico after the SPC watch was created and during the watch window. Quay County falls inside the watch bbox. Treat this as incident-report validation only until post-event satellite evidence is seeded and reviewed.

incident.name: Sparks Fire
incident.location: HWY 156 mile marker 41, Quay County, New Mexico

sources.name: NOAA SPC Day 2 Fire Weather Outlook
NM Fire Info: Sparks Fire Quay County Wildfire Notification
NM Fire Info: Sparks Fire Quay County Update 4/29/26
